libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: For tests that exercise Conscrypt socket/file-descriptor paths, configure both JVM test execution and native-image build args with: --add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED, --add-opens=java.base/java.net=ALL-UNNAMED, and --add-opens=java.base/sun.nio.ch=ALL-UNNAMED. Prefer local loopback sockets or in-memory SSLEngine handshakes; no external TLS service is required. Use Conscrypt.checkAvailability() and Conscrypt.newProvider() in tests so native library loading is exercised.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8879 [Automation] native-image run fails for org.conscrypt:conscrypt-openjdk-uber:1.4.1; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: The 1.4.1 artifact is old and its bundled native library may expose JDK 25/native-image compatibility issues that setup cannot fix.

Conscrypt extracts its bundled JNI library to a temporary/work directory at runtime; only set org.conscrypt.native.workdir if local verification shows the default temp directory is unavailable or mounted noexec.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra Maven dependency or Docker service is needed: conscrypt-openjdk-uber is self-contained and embeds its OpenJDK native libraries. Meaningful socket/platform coverage does need Java module opens because Conscrypt 1.4.1 reflectively accesses JDK socket internals.
